function: OT::CPAL::get_palette_colors(unsigned int, unsigned int, unsigned int*, unsigned int*) const
what_input_feature: The input must carry the exact font-table magic FOURCCs at fixed offsets: '_COLR' (5f 43 4f 4c 52) at 0x1b-0x1f and the 'hcts'-style tag bytes at 0x14-0x17, so that the harfbuzz table directory parses and palette_index stays < numPalettes to reach a populated CPAL table.

mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the CmpLogObserver buffer, finds the harfbuzz table-tag comparison operands, and substitutes the exact expected FOURCC (e.g. _COLR) at the matching input offsets — value_profile lacks I2SRandReplace and cannot place the literal magic bytes deterministically.

what_input_feature: The surrounding sfnt/CPAL header must be structurally valid enough (offset-table counts and CPAL version field non-zero) to pass sanitize and reach v1(); this is a multi-field gradient condition, not a single literal.
why_winner_satisfies: Winner seeds begin 00 01 00 00 00 01 (valid sfnt version + numTables) and carry consistent table offset/length bytes (00 00 00 18 / 00 01 08 ...) so CPAL::sanitize succeeds (W hits get_size and sanitize while L does not), allowing version!=0 to be observed.
why_loser_doesnt: The cmplog loser cannot incrementally improve the multi-field header toward validity because it lacks the CMP_MAP distance feedback that preserves near-matching structural inputs; its blocked seeds never accumulate the consistent offset/length structure.

what_input_feature: Input must carry valid font-table magic/tag bytes (e.g. a 'typ1' sfnt version word and a 'loca' table tag) so the loca/glyf tables parse and num_glyphs becomes large enough that a chosen gid is in range, letting gid >= num_glyphs evaluate false.
why_winner_satisfies: The vpc winner seeds open with the literal bytes 74 79 70 31 ('typ1') and contain 6c 6f 63 61 ('loca') at offset 0x0c — exact table-identifier byte sequences that I2SRandReplace plants from intercepted CMP operands so the table directory parses and num_glyphs is populated.
why_loser_doesnt: The value_profile loser lacks I2SRandReplace, so it never substitutes the exact 'typ1'/'loca' identifier bytes; its blocking seeds instead start with bytes like fe ff ff ff or e0 17 00 00 that do not form the table magic, leaving the glyph table unparsed and gid always >= num_glyphs (true branch).
mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the post-execution CmpLogObserver buffer, finds where the parser compared the input against the 'typ1'/'loca' identifiers, and substitutes the exact matching operand bytes into the input — value_profile has no such substitution stage, so vpc's I2S half is the technique that distinguishes the winner from the value_profile loser.

what_input_feature: Beyond the literal table tags, the input must drive the glyph-count / offset integer comparisons close enough to a valid in-range gid that the parser accepts the structure; this is the gradient-solvable numeric portion (num_glyphs and offset fields) rather than the exact-literal portion.
why_winner_satisfies: The vpc winner additionally exercises the CMP_MAP gradient: its avg hitcount on the false-branch path is 2 vs 0 for the cmplog loser, and it reaches the in-range gid so get_points runs (W hits 2 on the enclosing function), indicating the Hamming/prefix-distance feedback steered the numeric fields toward a valid count.
why_loser_doesnt: The cmplog loser has no CMP_MAP gradient feedback (it only buffers operands for I2S), so it cannot preserve inputs that get progressively closer on the num_glyphs/offset integer comparisons; it stays blocked 10/10 with avg hitcount 0 on the branch and never takes the false side.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP writes a (PC, operand-distance bucket) entry per CMP and rewards inputs whose operands are closer to matching, giving a hill-climbing gradient on the numeric glyph-count/offset comparisons that plain cmplog (I2S substitution only, no distance feedback) lacks — so vpc's value_profile half is what distinguishes the winner from the cmplog loser.

what_input_feature: A valid sfnt/OpenType container whose table directory names a 'CFF ' table (bytes 43 46 46 20 at the tag position) with a correctly-formed sfnt version and table offset, so this->blob->as<OT::cff1>() returns a real CFF table rather than the shared Null sentinel.
why_winner_satisfies: All four winner-resolving seeds (value_profile_cmplog, trial 1) carry the literal 'CFF ' (43 46 46 20) at offsets 0x0c-0x0f plus the sfnt magic 00 01 00 00 at 0x00-0x03 and a 00 00 00 18 table offset, exactly the byte set that lets the blob resolve to a non-Null OT::cff1 so line 1051 takes its false (winning) branch.
why_loser_doesnt: The loser-blocking seeds (value_profile, trial 1) lack the 'CFF ' tag at that offset (W shows 43(C)x4/46(F)x4 there while L shows 47(G)/4f(O)/53(S) and scattered bytes), so the blob never resolves to a CFF table and line 1051 always takes its true branch into { fini (); return; }.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog wins because its I2SRandReplace stage reads the table-tag comparison from the CmpLogObserver buffer and substitutes the exact 'CFF ' operand into the input at the tag offset; value_profile alone lacks I2SRandReplace and cannot place the exact 4-byte tag, while cmplog alone fails to bank the partially-correct sfnt header progress, so only the combined fuzzer reaches the false branch.

why_loser_doesnt: value_profile loser seeds are corrupt keyword fragments ('ac 45 4c 45 ...ELE', repeated ';;' runs) lacking the exact literal; without I2SRandReplace, value_profile cannot copy the parser's expected string constant directly into the input and stays blocked 10/10.
mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the CmpLogObserver buffer and substitutes the comparison's other operand (the expected SQL keyword literal) into the input at the matched offset; this I2S substitution is the delta value_profile lacks, supplying the exact keyword bytes that drive the parser to the false branch.

what_input_feature: A PRAGMA statement whose name-token byte sequence progressively approaches a real pragma name in the case-insensitive name table scanned by pragmaLocate (offsets 0x00-0x08 carrying 'PRAGMA ' plus a pragma-name prefix).
why_winner_satisfies: The vpc winner seed is the well-formed string 'PRAGMA Ostor;-' (offsets 0x00-0x06 = 50 52 41 47 4d 41 20), driving pragmaLocate's sqlite3StrICmp name comparisons toward a match; vpc carries the CMP_MAP gradient feedback that rewards inputs whose name bytes get closer to a table entry.
why_loser_doesnt: The cmplog loser has I2S but no CMP_MAP gradient, so it gets no partial-progress reward as a candidate pragma name converges; its blocking seeds are non-PRAGMA garbage like 'SELEEC' and '""/""' that never reach the pragma-name compare loop, and it blocks all 10 trials.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient (Hamming/prefix-distance buckets on the sqlite3StrICmp operand comparisons) preserves corpus inputs whose pragma-name token gets incrementally closer to a real table name; cmplog lacks this distance feedback, so it is the value_profile delta that separates vpc from cmplog.

what_input_feature: The exact pragma-name token bytes that equal a real entry in pragmaLocate's name table, so the StrICmp returns equal and the loop exits via the false (non-blocked-globally) path.
why_winner_satisfies: The vpc winner's ancestor chain contains an I2S-floor (dash) row at depth 6, evidence that I2SRandReplace substituted the compared pragma-name operand into the input; vpc has the CmpLogObserver buffer that lets I2S copy the literal expected name bytes into the 'PRAGMA ' token region.
why_loser_doesnt: value_profile has the CMP_MAP gradient but no I2SRandReplace stage, so it can only inch toward the name byte-by-byte and never lands the exact multi-byte token; it blocks 10/10 trials and its seeds (e.g. ';;\;;E<' and '.ELE.EL@E..CT') never form a valid pragma name.
mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the CmpLogObserver operand recorded at the sqlite3StrICmp call and substitutes the exact expected pragma-name bytes into the input; value_profile lacks I2S, so the I2S delta is what separates vpc from value_profile.

what_input_feature: The input must carry the exact ASCII token 'PRAGMA' at offset 0x00 (bytes 50 52 41 47 4d 41) so the parser dispatches into sqlite3Pragma and reaches the pragma-name comparison at 125998:7.
why_winner_satisfies: The winner-resolving seed begins '50 52 41 47 4d 41 20 4f 73 74 6f 72' = 'PRAGMA Ostor', presenting the verbatim PRAGMA keyword that the loser seeds (all SELECT/junk) never form.
why_loser_doesnt: Every loser-blocking seed is a SELECT-like or junk byte run ('SELEEC', '""/""', '.ELE.EL@E..CT') with no 'PRAGMA' token, so the pragma-name branch at 125998 is never entered (L shows T=0 F=20).
mechanism_attribution: Against value_profile (Pair 2 delta=I2S), only the winner has I2SRandReplace, which reads the CmpLogObserver buffer and substitutes the program's 'PRAGMA' keyword operand into the input at matching offsets; the winning seed's lineage carries an I2S-floor dash row at depth 6, evidence of an I2S find in its ancestry.

what_input_feature: Same feature: the literal 'PRAGMA' keyword (50 52 41 47 4d 41) at offset 0x00 gating entry into sqlite3Pragma and the true side of 125998:7.
why_winner_satisfies: The winner-resolving seed 'PRAGMA Ostor;-...' supplies the full keyword and reaches the branch (W shows T=1 F=1 at L125998), while cmplog's corpus stalls without it.
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog loser seeds ('SELEEC .`', '""/"" ') prefix-collide on no more than a couple of bytes with 'PRAGMA' and never accrete the full keyword, so they keep taking the false side (loser resolved=0/10, blocked=10).
mechanism_attribution: Against cmplog (Pair 1 delta=value_profile), only the winner adds the CMP_MAP gradient: each integer/string CMP writes a Hamming/prefix-distance bucket so an input whose prefix is closer to 'PRAGMA' than any seen before is preserved as corpus, giving a graded climb toward the keyword that plain cmplog edge-feedback lacks.

what_input_feature: The input must begin with the exact SQL keyword bytes 'PRAGMA' (50 52 41 47 4d 41) so the SQLite tokenizer emits the PRAGMA token that drives yy_reduce to this reduce action at 157969.
why_winner_satisfies: Both winner-resolving seeds carry the literal ASCII 'PRAGMA' at offsets 0x00-0x05 (PRAGMA Ostor / PRAGMA vdbe_), and only the winner takes the true branch (W T=2 F=17 vs L T=0 F=135 at L157969).
why_loser_doesnt: value_profile-discovered loser seeds are SELECT-family / punctuation garbage (e.g. ELE...CT, ;;\;;E<) with no contiguous PRAGMA keyword, so the tokenizer never emits the PRAGMA token and the true side stays at T=0.
mechanism_attribution: Adding I2S (cmplog's I2SRandReplace) to value_profile lets the mutator read the per-execution CMP buffer and substitute the keyword-matching bytes for 'PRAGMA' at the input head; the winning vpc seed's ancestor chain contains an I2S-floor dash row, confirming an I2S contribution that value_profile alone lacks.

what_input_feature: Same PRAGMA-keyword reduce target, but reaching it also requires progressively closing the comparison distance on the keyword/operand bytes so a near-match input is preserved as a corpus stepping stone.
why_winner_satisfies: Adding value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient to cmplog gives the winner the new-CMP_MAP-bucket feedback that retains partial-keyword inputs; the winner resolves 9/10 and reaches the branch (avg hitcount on branch winner=5), whereas plain cmplog does not.
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog-only loser seeds (SELEEC, ""/"") are short corrupted fragments discovered at 1-81s that block 10/10 (loser resolved=0/10 blocked=10) — without the CMP_MAP gradient cmplog cannot accrete the partial-keyword corpus needed to assemble PRAGMA.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP records a Hamming/prefix-distance bucket per CMP; an input whose operand pair is closer to matching the PRAGMA comparison adds a new bucket and is kept as corpus, supplying I2S with a better substitution substrate that cmplog alone cannot retain.
